items.headline: City Council approves digital signage amendment with Clear Channel Outdoor
items.summary: The council modified its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC regarding large format digital signage on city-owned property. This affects what advertising residents will see on municipal properties throughout San José.

items.headline: City seeks proposals for homeless encampment cleanup services
items.summary: The council reviewed a report on request for proposals for encampment cleanup services, indicating the city is contracting out these operations rather than handling them directly with city staff.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project moves forward
items.summary: The council took actions related to replacing elevators at the terminal, though specific details on scope and cost were not provided in the agenda summary.

items.headline: City negotiating property deal with school district on Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District over the purchase of property at 10480 Finch Avenue, with price and payment terms still being worked out. The outcome could affect what gets built or developed on that site.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, outlining how public spaces will be developed and connected in this area. This plan will shape recreational amenities and pedestrian infrastructure for residents over the coming years.

items.headline: City council updates internal procedures and protocols handbook
items.summary: The council made changes to its own operational procedures handbook. While procedural, this could reflect shifts in how the city conducts public business or manages meetings.

items.headline: Nine new homes proposed for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: The city is considering a plan to subdivide a 1-acre vacant lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This is a quasi-judicial hearing where the council will decide whether to approve the tentative map for the development.

items.headline: Supermicro invests $2.5M in equipment at Rock Avenue facility
items.summary: Major tech company Supermicro is installing equipment at its 988 Rock Avenue location in a $2.5 million project. This signals continued investment and operations expansion by one of San José's important tech manufacturers.
